questions.question_text: Answer the following questions CO Mapping a What is an array? Explain the differences between array and linked list. 2.5 b Define time and space complexity of an algorithm. Also, discuss the time-space trade-off in an algorithm. 2.5 c What is stack? Explain the Push and Pop operations with the help of a suitable example. 2.5 d Write an algorithm to implement a queue using linked list. 2.5 e What is a Binary search tree ? Create a BST for the list of elements 24, 5, 40, 10, 50 inserted in the order. 2.5 f Explain binary search algorithm. Also discuss its limitations. 2.5 g What is a graph data structure? Write adjacency list representation of the following graph: 2.5 h What is a hash function? Explain any one of the hash functions with the help of a suitable example. 2.5
Explain the circular queue data structure. Write algorithms for insertion and deletion in a circular queue. 10
Write an algorithm to create a two dimensional array V of size n × n such that V [i, j] = i - j for all i, j, 0≤i<n, 0≤j<n. Also find the sum of the elements of the array V. 10
Write a program/algorithm to create a singly linked list and display it in the reverse order. Also, do the time complexity analysis of performing the reverse operation. 10

Convert the following infix expression in prefix and postfix expression. Use the appropriate data structure and show all the intermediate steps. Also, draw the expression tree for the given infix expression. Infix expression: *C-D/E^F+ 10 B A F E D C

What are properties of a B-tree? Create the B-tree of order 4 for the following list of elements, inserted in the order showing all the intermediate steps. From the created B-Tree, delete the elements 3, 5, and 6. Arr[]= 15, 25, 5, 10, 40, 30, 35, 20, 3, 45, 6, 8 10

Write a program/algorithm for insertion sort and apply it to sort the following list of elements in ascending order. Show all the steps. Also, calculate the total number of comparison and swap operations performed while sorting the given list. Arr[] = 15, 24, 5, 4, 10, 35, 30, 8, 45, 1 10
Define minimum cost spanning tree . Write prim’s algorithm to create a spanning tree and apply it on the following graph to find the MCST. 10

What is a shortest path in a graph? Apply Dijkstra’s algorithm to find the shortest path from vertex ‘A’ to all other reachable vertices in the following graph. 10 B E D G A F C 4 1 6 5 2 2 3 3 4 3 4 5 7 B A C E F D 6 2 4 3 1 5 7 4 2
